entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal memory layer that retains user, session, and agent states across interactions. It utilizes a token-efficient single-pass extraction algorithm and hybrid retrieval to fuse semantic, keyword, and entity-linked signals.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'agent brain' features a self-wiring knowledge graph that automatically extracts entities and relationships from conversations. It combines vector retrieval with graph-based ranking and exposes over 30 MCP tools for seamless integration with Hermes and other coding agents.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a 'learn-by-doing' workflow through structured retain, recall, and reflect cycles for long-term agent improvement. It provides a simple two-line LLM wrapper for automatic memory management while ensuring per-user isolation via custom metadata filtering.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a stateful memory library designed to maintain evolving relationships between users, agents, and groups over time. It supports natural language querying of interaction histories and provides SDKs that format context for OpenAI-compatible environments.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness focuses on task success through iterative self-improvement and structured feedback loops. It generates persistent playbooks and artifacts that allow agents to inherit knowledge and strategies from previous complex assignments.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Hermes-LCM provides lossless context management by organizing message history into a hierarchical DAG structure for depth-aware compaction. This SQLite-backed plugin ensures no message is ever truly lost, offering tools for agents to grep and expand compacted context as needed.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project enables private, cross-session memory for Hermes Agent by providing a self-hosted backend for the Honcho library. It includes automated setup scripts for Docker and PostgreSQL, allowing users to maintain full control over their memory data using local or cloud LLMs.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates entirely on-device without cloud dependencies or API keys. It employs a sophisticated hybrid retrieval system using vector search, BM25, and causal graph traversal to share memory across different agent runtimes.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: ContextPilot focuses on inference efficiency by optimizing prefix reuse and deduplicating context to reduce prompt tokens by up to 36%. It is compatible with major engines like vLLM and SGLang, making it ideal for accelerating long-context agentic workloads.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer that stores information locally as open-format YAML 'engrams' to avoid API costs. It uses hybrid BM25 and embedding search to automate memory injection for Hermes and other developer-focused agents.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing codebases, documents, and media with automatic summarization and OCR capabilities. It uses edge tags to create bidirectional relationships between notes, accessible to agents via MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency, SQLite-backed memory system designed for sub-millisecond retrieval and storage operations. It utilizes a three-tier architecture evaluated on the BEAM long-context benchmark to provide high-performance local memory for Hermes.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: Developed for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, this anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs in real-time. It relies on local-first storage using SQLite-vec and provides an MCP-native server for automated configuration.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry-standard frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It enables Hermes to perform specialized tasks in cloud security and forensics while remaining compatible with the agentskills.io standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw introduces an 'Agentic Evolver' that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It is designed to unify skill libraries across multiple devices and sessions, offering native integration for Hermes and Codex.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate 36 distinct AI writing patterns and structural tells. It uses a comprehensive word replacement table and a two-pass audit process to ensure Hermes produces more natural, human-like prose.
entries.wondelai/skills: A cross-platform library that offers modular installation of skills for architecture, UX design, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI. It provides Hermes with a standardized framework for professional services compatible with the agentskills.io ecosystem.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills open format specifically for Pydantic AI. It optimizes Hermes' performance by using progressive disclosure of skill metadata to significantly reduce token consumption.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill allows Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions using specialized presets for machine learning and architecture. It features automated self-checking loops and supports advanced elements like animated connectors and tensor shapes.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-Hermes empowers the agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It helps Hermes identify structural conservation laws in codebases and generate reports on its own analysis limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink oracles, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It includes a local A/B testing framework to help developers evaluate and refine how Hermes handles smart contract data.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This skill enables literate programming by weaving together prose, diagrams, and source code into synchronized markdown files. It allows Hermes to extract runnable code from documentation and automatically sync edits back to the primary document.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus facilitates cross-instance shared memory, allowing Hermes to remember past work and train replacement models. It features an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality data and fine-tuning models based on agent activity.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry and SDK for managing the lifecycle of reusable AI skills with recursive dependency resolution. It uniquely integrates commerce rails, allowing for skill transactions using both USD and the TON blockchain.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide Hermes with standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX image generation models. They support precise technical requirements like hex color specification and structured JSON prompting.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both documentation and Python scaffolds on the fly, allowing Hermes to expand its own capabilities during use.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that coordinates multi-step missions using milestones and validation gates. It persists agent state in a disciplined on-disk model and generates handoff briefs to bridge separate sessions.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill focuses on the autonomous execution of complex, multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and recovery procedures. It provides Hermes with real-time progress tracking and rollback capabilities to ensure safe task completion.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading architecture to minimize token usage during tool discovery. Its socket-based daemon supports hot-reloading, maintaining persistent connections between Hermes and various MCP servers.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing code repositories and developer discourse into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant skills. It ensures high reliability by providing verifiable audit trails with file-and-line level provenance for every generated skill.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub serves as a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills with integrated security scanning and threat detection. It features an agent-to-agent feedback system to establish trust and reliability scores for shared skills.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project provides a C# and .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification with full MCP integration. It allows Hermes to leverage .NET resources through progressive disclosure and standardized YAML frontmatter.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill grants Hermes the ability to manage files, calendars, and contacts within a Nextcloud environment via WebDAV and CalDAV. It ensures secure communication through HTTPS and includes a guided setup script for credential validation.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages for a variety of AI coding agents, including Hermes, ensuring developers always have access to the latest builds. It features a unified proxy API for cross-provider compatibility and supports multi-agent orchestrators to manage complex, parallel workflows.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Hermesclaw focuses on high-security environments by implementing hardware-enforced sandboxing through the NVIDIA OpenShell kernel. It offers multi-layer protection that blocks unauthorized network egress and filesystem writes using pre-configured security policy presets.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This NixOS module enables declarative management of Hermes deployments, covering everything from systemd services to secure secret handling via sops-nix. It is ideal for users seeking a reproducible setup that supports both Nix-managed skills and interactive CLI installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent comes pre-loaded with 100 tools and local model support via LM Studio. It eliminates the need for Docker or complex dependencies, offering a portable GUI-based environment with a dynamic tool maker for custom automation.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker sandbox image that includes the official Hermes installation and mini-swe-agent. It is built for simplicity, supporting persistent state through mounted directories and allowing users to target specific Hermes tags during the build process.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a centralized Docker-first control plane for managing multiple agent instances and workspaces through a single web application. It features hierarchy-aware delegation between agents and automated task scheduling with integrated support for Telegram and WhatsApp.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production-ready headless environments, this repository configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is optimized for autonomous execution, enabling the agent to perform recurring AI cron jobs without manual intervention.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal, allowing users to interact with the agent directly through a browser. It is designed to be deployed as a Portainer Git Repository stack, utilizing GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: A self-hosted orchestration platform that manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It integrates the Aegis review system to provide human-in-the-loop oversight and spend monitoring for complex task dispatching.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: This framework enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with visual organizational charts and durable memory across 23+ providers, including Hermes and Ollama. It distinguishes itself by allowing agents to learn new skills directly from conversation history while managing delegation within the swarm.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: A specialized meta-optimization tool that uses an automated outer-loop search to evolve and improve harness code for Hermes Agent. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: An agent-vs-agent arena where two competing AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, battle for GitHub stars. The platform features a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution and scoreboard metrics as nine autonomous agents execute code and learning cycles.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: A comprehensive pipeline that coordinates 17 specialized agents through a master orchestrator to handle research, implementation, and infrastructure tasks. It enforces strict quality control by requiring mandatory sign-offs from dedicated reviewer and tester agents at every stage.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: An AI-native software development lifecycle (SDLC) framework that provides Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It automates an eight-phase workflow, using graph-diff gates to ensure architectural integrity from initial planning to final health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: A delegation-focused framework that routes tasks between Hermes, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing context pollution through isolated workflows. It prioritizes operational safety by enforcing configurable timeouts and strict output character limits during agent handoffs.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: A Rust-based CLI tool that tracks token usage and costs across various AI coding agents, including Hermes. It features a native TUI and 3D contribution graphs to visualize resource consumption.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: A collection of community-driven CLI themes that allow users to customize the Hermes terminal UI. Using YAML configurations, developers can modify ASCII banners, colors, and labels to create styles ranging from cyberpunk to retro DOS.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: A port of the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel. This integration eliminates the need for OAuth or external processes while adding MLOps tools for RL training and vLLM inference.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous agent control via MCP, providing a visible UI for human supervision. It supports durable state management through named sessions and can infer structured schemas from web pages.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: A TAW Agent framework that enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents with per-folder configurations. It allows developers to seamlessly switch between default and project-local memories, tools, and instructions.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A deterministic static linter that validates AI agent configs, system prompts, and tool descriptions without requiring LLM calls. It integrates into CI/CD pipelines to detect structural failure modes and schema-intent mismatches using HERM v1.1 scoring.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: A specialized migration utility designed to transition users from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent. The tool maps model configurations to Hermes YAML format and transfers personas, memories, and credentials.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: An automation suite that deploys a full Hermes Agent stack using Docker, LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant. It provides a zero-cost setup including 29 community plugins for memory and quality validation.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This lightweight Python and vanilla JavaScript interface provides full feature parity with the Hermes CLI in a mobile-friendly web format. It features a three-panel layout that allows users to browse workspace files and view inline previews alongside their chat sessions.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard designed for multi-platform management, supporting unified configuration for eight channels including Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. It offers real-time chat with session persistence and detailed analytics for tracking token usage and costs across different platforms.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace serves as a multi-agent control plane capable of role-based dispatch and swarm orchestration. It integrates a Monaco-based file manager and a dedicated memory browser, allowing users to search and edit agent context directly within the UI.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway for managing the Hermes stack that features an integrated xterm.js terminal with full PTY support. It provides granular control over individual agent profiles and detailed token analytics to monitor costs and model performance.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: This native macOS workspace uses direct SSH connections to ensure the remote host remains the single source of truth for all agent data. The interface consolidates sessions, Kanban boards, and cron job management into a profile-aware desktop environment.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React dashboard designed specifically as a Telegram Mini App for managing agents on the go. It supports background spawning of up to five concurrent instances and includes system monitoring gauges for CPU, memory, and disk usage.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows GUI that runs without administrative rights or Docker, focusing on local model integration via LM Studio. It comes pre-loaded with over 100 tools for TTS and ComfyUI, plus a dynamic tool maker for creating custom API wrappers at runtime.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the initial setup process with a guided installer for local dependencies and environment configuration. It provides a unified GUI to manage 16 messaging gateways and 14 toolsets, featuring full-text search for all session histories.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides a real-time activity feed and cost tracking for active Hermes processes. It utilizes FTS5 search for deep inspection of conversation histories and allows for the management of scheduled cron jobs.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-first workspace that emphasizes extensibility through an integrated marketplace for discovering skills and MCP servers. It features tool execution timelines and profile-scoped management of global memory and agent configurations.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: This glassmorphic React application uses a Kanban-style task board to track active tool work and subagent progress. It combines a lightweight Python proxy with an integrated file browser and terminal for workspace-aware project management.
